prompt: Explain the restaurant onboarding flow end-to-end. Name the exact frontend pages, backend endpoints, and database models involved. Be specific with file paths.
Explain how customer authentication works in this codebase. Trace the flow from login form submission through token generation and storage. Name all files involved.
Explain the WhatsApp webhook integration. How does an incoming WhatsApp message get processed, stored, and trigger notifications? List all files in the chain.

A restaurant owner reports that updating their location on the settings page silently fails — the form submits but the old address persists on refresh. Diagnose the likely cause and identify the exact files to fix.
Customers report that their order history page sometimes shows orders from other customers. Identify the most likely backend files and endpoints where this data leakage could originate.
The admin dashboard analytics endpoint returns a 500 error when a restaurant has zero orders. Trace the analytics calculation code and identify where a division-by-zero or null reference might occur.

Design a restaurant menu item bulk import feature that accepts a CSV file. List which existing files need modification, what new files are needed, and how it integrates with the current menu management system.

Design a customer loyalty points system. Identify where points should be earned (order completion), stored (database model), displayed (customer portal), and redeemed (checkout flow). Map to existing files.
Design a real-time order tracking feature for the customer portal. Identify the existing WebSocket infrastructure, the order status model, and which frontend components need new tracking UI.
The API client code is duplicated across the restaurant portal and customer portal. Identify all API client/helper files in both frontends and propose a shared module structure.
Identify all authentication-related middleware in the backend. Are there redundant auth checks? Propose a consolidated middleware chain with the exact files to modify.
Evaluate the current database model structure. Identify all SQLAlchemy models, their relationships, and any missing indexes or N+1 query risks in the API endpoints that use them.
Map the complete notification system architecture. How do notifications get created, stored, delivered (push/websocket/email), and displayed? Identify gaps in the current implementation.
The restaurant portal loads slowly on initial page load. Identify the main data-fetching hooks, their API calls, and which ones might be making redundant or waterfall requests on mount.

WebSocket connections are dropping intermittently for restaurant users. Identify the WebSocket setup code in both frontend and backend, the reconnection logic, and potential causes for disconnections.
